title: Asynchronous Architecture and Pattern Selection · Compiler Case: Choreography Orchestration
summary: A request path adds a broker without a latency or failure reason, hides synchronous coupling behind RPC messages, and uses one global queue for work distribution, broadcasting, and replay. Planned artifact: Add and verify the choreography orchestration increment in pattern selection and coupling decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose choreography or orchestration by workflow visibility, policy ownership, compensation, evolution, and failure diagnosis needs.
Evaluate request/reply over messaging against ordinary HTTP using correlation, timeout, cancellation, failure ambiguity, and capacity evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Choreography Orchestration · Theory Choreography Orchestration
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 3013 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose HTTP, work queue, pub/sub, or retained stream from explicit coupling needs; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Choreography Orchestration contract for the supplied data.
